text: Evan's Thanksgiving Turkey: Evan humorously claimed he "stuffed the turkey" at Thanksgiving, which Matt acknowledged with approval, showcasing their lighthearted banter about holiday traditions.
Matt's Tax Woes: Matt shared his frustration about spending 40% of his end-of-year bonus on overdue taxes, leading to a joke about possibly moving to Colombia with Dave to avoid the IRS.

Reed's Test: Matt praised Reed for passing a "test" where he resisted the temptation to party on a Sunday Funday, humorously expressing concern that Reed might fall victim to alcohol and neglect his work responsibilities.

Oppenheimer Movie Joke: Matt made a humorous reference to a fictional scene in Oppenheimer where a character activates a "personal fusion reactor vest," showcasing the group's penchant for absurd humor.

Maine Red Claws: Matt lamented the name change of the Maine Red Claws to the Maine Celtics in 2022, expressing his sadness over the loss of the original name, which reflects his interest in local sports teams.
Skipping Rocks: Matt noted that Reed is unnaturally good at skipping rocks, which seems to be a surprising talent of his.
Foamy New Year: Reed made it only 28 minutes into 2024 before getting "foamy," a result of Nigel forgetting to put out a foam bucket, despite Reed's New Year's resolution to avoid it.
Tech Preferences: Matt and Reed both expressed reluctance to buy new tech until Google or Samsung release their versions, with Matt humorously stating he’s not an "Apple plebian" except for work.
Gaming Commitment: Reed was hesitant to buy a game that everyone else was excited about, leading to a playful backlash from Nick, who questioned Reed's decision.
Work vs. Partying: Matt expressed concern that Reed might neglect his work responsibilities for partying, but Reed ultimately went to work, leading Matt to commend him for passing the "test."

Dave's Tax Strategy: Dave joked about potentially moving to Colombia to evade the IRS, suggesting that he could enjoy a luxurious lifestyle while working poolside and paying Colombian taxes on a US salary.

Incan Customs Checkpoint: Dave shared an interesting historical tidbit about a sacred city being a customs checkpoint between the Andes and Amazon, humorously referring to it as "fucking incan ICE border porter."

Dave's Weekend Plans: Dave humorously described his Easter weekend as being spent playing video games, smoking weed, and drinking soy milk while his "alpha girlfriend" worked, highlighting a laid-back lifestyle.
Chili's Pizza Video: Matt asked Evan if he still had the Chili's pizza video, indicating it's a memorable or funny moment they all want to revisit.
Thanksgiving Turkey Stuffing: Evan humorously mentioned that he "stuffed the turkey at Thanksgiving," suggesting he might have a knack for cooking or a funny story related to it.

Ev Boy Jr.: There was a playful discussion about Evan's child, with jokes about gender identity and Evan's role in supporting their transition, showcasing the group's lighthearted banter around parenting.
Basketball Bias: Evan defended a basketball play, insisting it wasn't a moving pick because the defender was short, leading to a humorous exchange about biases in sports opinions.
Amortization Lesson: Nick jokingly asks Nigel to explain amortization to the group, leading to Matt's enthusiastic call for Nigel's expertise.

Political Identity: Reed humorously claims Matt is "brain damaged" and believes in 10 genders, while Matt identifies as "socially liberal and fiscally conservative," prompting Nick to suggest that's a conservative stance.
Gaming Laptop Inquiry: Matt seeks Nigel's advice on a potential gaming laptop purchase, showing his reliance on Nigel for tech recommendations.
Helldivers Gaming Plans: Matt invites Nigel to play Helldivers together, and Nigel agrees but mentions he usually plays late at night, indicating their shared interest in gaming.
Performance Monitoring Tools: Nigel shares his preferred apps for monitoring and optimizing gaming performance, showcasing his tech-savvy nature within the group.
Investment Advice: Matt humorously criticized Nick Rondinone's investment decision, calling it a "really bad investment," which led to Nick jokingly agreeing with Matt's assessment.
Helldivers Revelation: Matt confronted Nick about secretly playing Helldivers, to which Nick denied it, claiming Nigel was lying about him.

Back-to-Back Nattys: Matt congratulated Nick on winning back-to-back national championships, while Nick credited Tom Lovkay and Dorian Tanasi for the success, noting that Matt profited from their performances.
Haunted House: Matt reminisced about the first time he got high with Nick and Mike, referencing their haunted house, which both Whitney and Taylor confirmed was "haunted af."
Boston Homelessness: Matt and Nick discussed the harsh realities of homelessness in Boston, with Matt noting the brutal conditions and lack of adequate shelters for those in need.
Stacy's Guy Friend: Reed was unable to get bail and joked about asking Stacy's guy friend for money, but found out he was in Oklahoma City and didn't have Venmo.
Interest Rate Joke: When Reed asked for money, Matt humorously offered to lend it to him at a 33.33% interest rate, implying that Stacy wouldn't bail him out.
Mackin' on Benataris: Reed mentioned that Stacy can tell when he's been flirting with other girls ("benataris"), suggesting a playful dynamic in their relationship.
Stacy's Odds: Matt joked that Stacy at -160 against him in a 1v1 matchup was easy money, implying he thinks he could easily win against her.
Ditching Stacy: Matt teased Reed about not being able to ditch Stacy for the night, indicating that their relationship is serious enough that Reed can't just leave her behind.
Memorial Day Weekend Plans: Matt mentioned that Reed and Stacy would be joining him and others at a golf resort on Lake Champlain, highlighting their social plans together.
Lake George Getaway: Matt called out Reed for wanting to do a date night after just having a getaway with Stacy to Lake George, suggesting Reed's expectations might be too high.
Golf Course Recommendation: Matt recommended a golf course in Nahant, saying Reed and Stacy would love it, indicating that they enjoy spending time together at fun locations.
Dicky's AIM Incident: Nic and Nick recall a time when Matt sent a revealing photo on AIM, which has left a lasting impression on Nic's memory.
Cock Torture Bar: During a conversation about a bar, Nic humorously laments receiving a lot of "cock and balls" references, leading to Reed teasing him about his name being "DICK[Y]."

Cum Transformation: Nic humorously describes a fictional transformation where Matt becomes a "cum boy," leading to a deep philosophical moment about their origins, which Matt embraces with humor on his birthday.

Florida Bachelor Party: Matt jokingly brings up a questionable encounter involving a woman at a bachelor party in Florida, which Nic denies, adding to the group's inside jokes about Dicky's escapades.

Bathroom Peace: Matt expressed the need for a hotel during a wedding trip, emphasizing the challenge of sharing two bathrooms among eight people, especially for the ladies, and his desire for a 25-minute bathroom break in peace.
Moving Preferences: Matt revealed that he wants to move to Seattle, but Katie prefers Chicago, highlighting their differing aspirations for relocation.
Baby Weight Confusion: During a conversation about a baby's weight, both Matt and Katie independently commented on a mix-up regarding the baby's actual weight, showcasing their synchronized humor.
Granby News: Matt mentioned that Kaycee is always plugged into local news, as she was the first to inform Katie about Maus's death, drawing a parallel to another local incident involving Lila.

Solo Weekend Plans: With Katie going to Vermont, Matt planned to spend the weekend alone watching the Bruins and playing video games with his brother's friends, indicating his typical weekend routine when Katie is away.

Harvey and Marvin's Health Issues: Matt's cats, Harvey and Marvin, had stomach issues, with Harvey suffering from diarrhea and Marvin vomiting, leading to a series of vet visits and concerns about Marvin's potential heart disease.
Poop Snuggles: Matt humorously mentioned that Marvin enjoys "poop snuggles," where he jumps on Matt's lap while he uses the bathroom, prompting Dave to express surprise at this revelation.
Flight Cancellation for Cats: Matt canceled his flights to Springfield to stay home with Harvey when he got sick, highlighting his dedication as a cat dad.

Tim and Wom's Birthday Weekend: Matt organized a trip to Bluff Point, a golf resort on Lake Champlain, for Tim and Wom's birthdays, where they enjoyed a beautiful 18-hole course and had cabins for the weekend.

Wet Dream Reference: During a conversation about their trip to Plattsburgh, Matt reminded Mike about his infamous wet dream from a previous stay, leading to a humorous exchange about reliving that night.
Wom's Strip Club Curiosity: While in Vermont, Wom asked a lot of questions about strip clubs, which made Katie feel awkward, highlighting Wom's curious nature.
Fenway Freebies: Matt is attending a Red Sox vs. Yankees game for free with Ian, expressing disdain for the Sox ownership and predicting a Yankees victory before heading to watch a Celtics game.
Zombies Session: Matt had a nice gaming session with Ian playing zombies, noting they never finished all the Easter eggs, highlighting their shared gaming history.
MAGA Encounter: At a game, Matt encountered a MAGA supporter who bought beers for everyone except him and Ian, which he found disheartening despite the crowd's enjoyment of the guy.
Gaming Party: Matt was trying to gather friends for gaming, mentioning he was with Ian and Mike Z, while Nic expressed he couldn't play with Mike Z due to past issues involving Nic's friend.
Mudpie Incident: Matt humorously described using Mike's high-pressure shower head after making a "big old mudpie," indicating a messy bathroom experience.
Mike's Birthday: The group celebrated Mike Christensen's birthday, with Matt expressing joy at seeing everyone together and wishing him well.

Fatherhood Update: Mike shared that fatherhood is going well despite some challenges with his newborn's circumcision and tongue tie, but noted that the baby is gaining weight and they are getting decent sleep.
Brady Rings: Matt humorously compares his esophageal rings from a doctor's appointment to Tom Brady's Super Bowl rings, showcasing his knack for making light of awkward situations.
AIM Incident: Nick humorously recalls a time when Matt sent a revealing photo on AIM, which Nic claims is forever burned into his memory.
Cock Torture Bar: During a conversation about images, Nic jokingly laments that he seems to always get the "cock and balls" content, leading to Reed teasing him about his name being "DICK[Y]."
Cum Boy Transformation: Nic crafted a humorous and elaborate message celebrating Matt's birthday, referring to him as a "cum boy," which prompted deep reflections from the group about their origins.

Poo-Flinger Fiasco: Matt reminded Nic of a past incident where he tried to pass off a catapult as his own invention called the "Poo-Flinger" in 8th grade, resulting in an F, which Dave humorously claims was revolutionary.

Boys Trip Debate: Matt expressed concern that the ladies would be upset about not seeing them on the boys trip, emphasizing that if it’s just guys, they should go somewhere with a strip club instead of Steamboat Springs.
Munkstock Invitation: Matt invites everyone to Munkstock, emphasizing that everyone is welcome except for Jesse, who is humorously excluded by Dave.
Bluff Point Golf Resort: Matt is planning a trip to Bluff Point, a golf resort on Lake Champlain, for Wom's birthday, and he intends to "Crack 90" on the course with Reed as his witness.
Wet Dream Reference: Nick humorously reminds Matt about a past incident in Plattsburgh where Mike had a wet dream, suggesting Matt should tease Mike about it during the trip.
Kiss Bet: Nick jokingly bets that if Matt breaks 90 at Bluff Point, he must kiss Reed with tongue, adding a humorous twist to the golf challenge.
Christmas Gift Tradition: Matt gives Wom and Tim a weekend stay at Bluff Point every year, highlighting a tradition of family gatherings at the golf resort.
Communication Issues: During a gaming session, Matt jokes about getting Stacy involved to resolve a communication issue with Reed, referencing their ongoing banter about Bluff Point.
NOLA Casino Luck: Matt claims that the casino in New Orleans is the only casino where he has never lost money, contrasting with his usual gambling experiences.
Degenerate in NOLA: Reed and Nick express a desire to witness Matt's antics in New Orleans, with Reed humorously suggesting they should see him in a strip club for just 10 minutes.
Katie's Visit: Matt mentions being attracted to Katie but humorously notes that she will be on her period during their trip, which complicates his plans.
Seafood and Beignets: Reed jokingly advises Matt to focus on eating seafood and beignets while in New Orleans instead of gambling.

Mommy Milkers: During a conversation about preferences, Matt humorously declared his love for "Mommy Milkers" over Sicilian deep dish pizza, leading to a playful exchange about seeing "Mommy Milkers" in Atlantic City.

Prayer for Lost Funds: After losing $750 gambling in Atlantic City, Matt prayed for the return of his lost money and subsequently received an email about an extra cash payment from SAP that matched his loss, prompting him to praise the lord.
Rondy's Epic Journey: Matt recounted how Rondy flew 6060 miles and endured a 700-mile car ride with him to Atlantic City, highlighting the lengths friends will go for each other.
AC Trip Plans: Matt expressed a strong desire to visit Atlantic City again, suggesting it as a destination for a guys trip and mentioning an AMEX offer for spending at the Hard Rock Atlantic City.
Group Trip Enthusiasm: Matt and Nick discussed the possibility of a group trip to Atlantic City, with Matt noting that only a few friends, including Nick and Reed, would likely join him.
Matt's Sister's Wedding: Matt has a sister's wedding in Buffalo from September 20th-23rd, which he mentions while considering his schedule for the month.
Paxlovid Prescription: Matt is supposed to get prescribed Paxlovid but hasn't received it yet, which is a concern since he has a 6.5-hour drive to Buffalo for his sister's wedding.

Family Christmas Gatherings: Matt explains that his family waits until everyone is together for Christmas, leading to him celebrating with them on the 27th after his sister and Ben were in Buffalo until the 26th.
Martha's Vineyard Camping: Matt mentioned camping in an Airstream before heading to Martha's Vineyard, emphasizing that they were staying disconnected from the internet during the trip.

Wet Dream in Plattsburgh: Matt reminded Mike about the time he had a wet dream while they stayed in Plattsburgh on their way to Canada for the GWC, which seems to be a memorable and possibly embarrassing moment for Mike.
Barcelona Trip: Matt and Katie planned a trip to Spain and Portugal, including stops in Barcelona, Madrid, Porto, and Lisbon in October.
Christopher Columbus Statue: During his time in Barcelona, Matt expressed frustration about the statue of Christopher Columbus still standing, questioning why it hasn't been taken down.
Train Experience: Matt described the train ride from Barcelona to Madrid as an awesome experience and mentioned plans to stop in either Sintra or Cascais on the way to Lisbon.
Culinary Highlights: Matt humorously noted that the best part about Spain was the "MUCHAS TATAS GRANDE," referring to the large potatoes, which led to a pun with "paTATAS BRAVAS" from Dave.

Vermont Strip Club: During a trip to Vermont, Matt mentioned that Wom was asking a lot of questions about a strip club, which made Katie feel awkward, leading to the punchline about "tight lips gweeod" from Dave.
Plattsburgh Night: Nick referenced a "fateful night" in high school related to Plattsburgh, hinting at an embarrassing moment involving Mike's wet dream.

Bluff Point Golf Resort: Matt described Bluff Point on Lake Champlain as a beautiful golf resort where he and Tim have had memorable weekends, complete with golf carts and cabins, making it an annual gift for them.

Christmas Tradition: Matt has a tradition of gifting Tim and Wom a weekend stay at the Bluff Point golf resort on Lake Champlain every year, highlighting the importance of family and shared experiences.
Scooter Gang Nostalgia: Matt reminisced about the days of "Scooter Gang," highlighting the fun times they had, while Nigel mentioned that Nic's razor scooter is legendarily lost in the woods.

Lime Scooter Controversy: Nick joked that based on Matt's reckless use of Lime scooters, they might be considered "pro-genocide," to which Matt humorously responded that he'd run down ICE agents or protestors while under the influence.
Man of Honor: Matt was designated as his sister's "Man of Honor" for her wedding, while his brother was the "Bride's Man," highlighting the unconventional roles at the event.
COVID Wedding Drama: Matt tested positive for COVID just before his sister's wedding, leading to a frantic search for Paxlovid to prevent transmission, especially to elderly family members.

Outdoor Wedding Precautions: Despite his COVID diagnosis, Matt managed to attend the outdoor wedding after taking Paxlovid and being cautious, ensuring he didn't spread the virus to anyone, especially his at-risk grandfather.
Rondy bach loan joke: Matt jokingly suggested he would take out a loan in Katie's name to fund Rondy's bachelor party, referencing the group's ongoing loan jokes and Papa Gweeod's financial antics.
Gweeod the Scooter Bandit: Reed was humorously accused of being framed by the Austin police alongside "Gweeod the scooter bandit," referencing a running joke about reckless behavior.

Memoir Idea: Matt proposed writing a memoir titled "Chopped Salads Should Be Eaten With A Spoon, but They're Only Ever Served With A Fork: A Memoir by Gweeod," which would compile his grievances about the world.
Group Name Change: Nick Rondinone changed the group name to “No One Games With Gweeod Anymore,” leading to a playful discussion about who actually games with Matt.
Loan Joke: Matt joked about taking out a loan in Katie's name to fund Rondy's bachelor party, referencing the group's ongoing loan jokes and Matt's willingness to go to extremes for friends.
Group Name Change: Matt changed the group name to “No One Games Anymore,” highlighting the group's decline in gaming activity.
Gweeod Reference: Nick Rondinone humorously changed the group name to “No One Games With Gweeod Anymore,” poking fun at Matt's gaming habits.
Dave's Gaming Status: Matt joked that the group name should be "No One Besides Dave Games with Gweeod Anymore," indicating that Dave is the only one still actively gaming.

Chopped Salad Memoir: Matt proposed writing a memoir titled "Chopped Salads Should Be Eaten With A Spoon, but They're Only Ever Served With A Fork: A Memoir by Gweeod," which would compile his grievances about the world and his ideal solutions.
Quad Gaming: Matt shared a "Dear Diary" moment about finally getting a gaming quad together with SkrimpDr, animalmother, and 420cheesin, emphasizing the fun they had despite not winning any games.

Kristi Noem SHIELD Theory: Matt humorously claimed that Kristi Noem is now running SHIELD, the fictional Marvel organization, after leaving DHS, leading to a playful exchange with Nick about the absurdity of the idea.
Loan for Rondy's Bachelor Party: Matt joked about taking out a loan in Katie's name to fund Rondy's bachelor party, referencing "Papa Gweeod" as a humorous example of financial irresponsibility.

Reed's Instruction Request: Reed humorously asked for instructions on how to have sex for two hours, prompting a discussion about foreplay and positions, with Matt emphasizing the importance of variety.

Matt's Confession: Matt admitted he exaggerated about the duration of his sexual encounters, revealing it was actually 30 minutes of foreplay and an hour of sex, leading to Reed's shocked reaction and Nick's comment about Reed looking up to Matt.

Joey Chestnut Conspiracy: While tripping on acid, Matt claimed that Joey Chestnut runs a "Food World Record Crime Syndicate" that controls all information about food eating records, specifically alleging that the true world record for popcorn consumption is hidden.

Popcorn Record Debate: Matt argued that the record for most popcorn consumed is absurdly low at 32 servings of 28 ounces in 8 minutes, suggesting that someone has eaten more but it's obscured by Chestnut's syndicate.
Nick's Joke: Nick humorously asked if Joey Chestnut holds the record for "most cum guzzled," to which Matt quipped that it's actually Rod Stewart.

MarkGuiodFAA Humor: Matt expressed that he doesn't find the MarkGuiodFAA humor funny and prefers jokes about Reed becoming his new dad, revealing his complicated relationship with his own father and the anxiety it brings him.
Birthday Confusion: On Reed's birthday, Matt humorously noted he couldn't hang out because it was also his dad's birthday, prompting Reed to joke about watching MarkGuiodFAA's OnlyFans livestream.

Acid Conspiracy: While tripping on acid, Matt concocted a wild theory that Joey Chestnut is running a "Food World Record Crime Syndicate" to control information about food eating records, claiming that the true record for popcorn consumption is hidden.
Rick's Cabaret Experience: Matt spent two nights at Rick's Cabaret while on acid, leading to the question of whether a strip club is more or less fun under the influence.
Weekend Plans: Matt planned to take acid the weekend after a hockey game with his sister and grandpa, humorously clarifying he wouldn't be on acid for that family outing.
Drug-Fueled Weekend: Matt mentioned a weekend filled with various drugs, including molly, coke, and acid, prompting Nic to express concern and remind everyone to test the coke for safety.
SAP Work Visa Dream: Matt joked about potentially moving to Colombia with Dave to avoid taxes, suggesting he could get a work visa through SAP and enjoy a luxurious lifestyle while working poolside.

Customer Success Money: Matt mentioned making "Customer Success money" and noted that he wouldn't buy Nic a laptop because he already bought himself one, highlighting the stress of his current role compared to his previous position in Sales.

Prayer for Lost Funds: After losing $750 in Atlantic City, Matt humorously prayed for his money back and received an unexpected email from SAP about an extra cash payment that matched his loss, leading him to exclaim "Praise the lord!".

Tony's Expertise: Matt praised Nigel's dad, Tony, for his extensive knowledge about ERP and various SAP systems, calling him a genius and expressing admiration for the conversation they had about complex systems.

Job Security with SAP: Matt confidently stated that the military industrial complex runs on SAP, which he believes gives him job security, while also humorously downplaying Reed's role in the industry.
Matt's Work Setup: Matt works primarily from bed and finds it challenging without multiple monitors, expressing frustration about his work laptop being a MacBook Pro, which he refers to disdainfully.

Work Boredom: Matt openly shares his boredom with work, stating that his job has become simple and low stakes, reflecting on how he and Katie's financial situation has changed from feeling upper middle class to struggling with financial freedom.

Anxiety and Concert Conflict: Matt reveals he has crippling anxiety that prevents him from flying or working from his personal laptop, which complicates his plans to attend a Blink 182 concert due to VPN issues with his work systems.
Swampscott Smashburger: Matt had a bad experience with a smash burger in Swampscott, claiming it led to "sloppy shits" and deemed the Agave relish too exotic for his taste.

Chopped Salads Memoir: Matt humorously announced his intention to write a memoir titled "Chopped Salads Should Be Eaten With A Spoon, but They're Only Ever Served With A Fork," expressing his frustrations with food presentation norms.
Pizza Love: Matt declared pizza as his "desert island food," emphasizing his deep affection for it during a conversation about how many pizzas are too many.

Sicilian Style Deep Dish: In a political discussion, Matt passionately advocated for Sicilian Style Deep Dish pizza, linking it to concerns about potential deportations affecting his Italian relatives.
Reed's Gut Biome: Reed claims he has a bad gut biome and gets "foamy" when drinking, prompting Matt to suggest he should switch from beer to bourbon.
Grandpa's Favorite Beer: Matt shared a moment of remembrance with a Coor's Light, stating it was his grandpa's favorite beer as a way to send him off.
Drunk Softball Players: Matt described the post-softball crowd as a nuisance, noting they trash the place and yell at people, implying they might not be the most polite drinkers.

Rooster Incident: Matt referenced a law about happy hour drinks being influenced by a state senator's spoiled kid who got drunk and caused a fatal accident, emphasizing the absurdity of the situation.

Maine Celtics: Matt shared that the GLeague affiliate of the Celtics, previously known as the Maine Red Claws, changed their name to the Maine Celtics in 2022, expressing his dismay with crying emojis.
Celtics vs. Rangers: During a game night, Matt joked about the Celtics "munting" the whole game, prompting him to ask the group to look up the meaning of "munting."
Sports Betting: Nic questioned Matt's interest in sports betting after he lamented wasting time watching the Celtics lose, indicating a shared skepticism about their performance.

Patriots Perfect Season: Nick reminded Matt of the Patriots' perfect season, lamenting that it was ruined by New York, to which Matt responded with disappointment but noted the Pats have won three titles since then.
Patriots Game Haunting: While in Martha's Vineyard, Matt expressed frustration that Joey Chestnut was at a Patriots game in Cincinnati, humorously claiming he was being haunted by him.
Gaming After Pats: Matt mentioned he would be gaming all day and night after the Patriots game, indicating a strong connection between his gaming schedule and the team's performance.
Captain Itchy Cock: Nic jokingly referred to Matt as "Captain Itchy Cock" after Matt was late logging on to game, which led to Nic's frustration about waiting for him.

Red Sox Rivalry: Matt passionately expresses his disdain for the Yankees, claiming they "suck" and that he prefers to see the Red Sox winning while the Yankees lose, highlighting his loyalty to Boston teams.
Fenway Trip: Matt mentions going to Fenway Park for a game, indicating he has a free ticket and plans to leave early to catch a Celtics game afterward, showcasing his dedication to multiple sports.
Historical Baseball Knowledge: Matt shares trivia about the Cincinnati Red Stockings being established before the Boston Red Sox, demonstrating his deep knowledge of baseball history and rivalries.
Missing Games: Matt laments missing a Red Sox game in Denver by just one day, indicating his commitment to following the team closely and his tendency to plan poorly around sports events.
Jack Edwards Incident: Reed referenced a moment when Jack Edwards publicly called someone fat and had to apologize, highlighting the drama surrounding Bruins commentary.
Bruins vs. Pens: Matt expressed happiness after the Bruins defeated the Penguins, indicating his loyalty to the team and the rivalry.
Doghouse Reference: Nic joked about being in the doghouse after a game, implying that his partner was upset with him for watching the Bruins lose.
Competitive Series Hope: Matt expressed hope for a competitive second round in the playoffs, showing his passion for the Bruins' performance.
Nick's Anti-Bruins Sentiment: Nick repeatedly declared his disdain for the Bruins, emphasizing the rivalry and playful banter within the group.
Solo Bruins Night: Matt mentioned spending a night alone watching the Bruins and playing video games while Katie was away, showcasing his dedication to the team.
UVM Hockey Championship: Matt celebrated the University of Vermont Catamounts winning the National Championship for the first time in his life, expressing his excitement with "Holy shit boys!"

BU vs UVM Hockey Game: Matt frequently references attending games, including a specific instance where he was going to see BU take on the UVM Catamounts in the Hockey East playoffs, emphasizing his enthusiasm with "HOCKEY!" and "EAAAAAAST."
Man of Honor: Matt's sister is having him as her "Man of Honor" at her wedding, while his brother will be the "Bride's Man," indicating a unique and modern wedding setup.
Reed's Double Cross: Reed pretended to skip work to party, but actually went to work, leading Matt to commend him for passing the "test" of responsibility, while Nick called it a "double cross."

UCONN Bet: Matt confidently placed a bet on UCONN to win the National Championship, claiming he made money off them the previous year and is so sure they'll repeat that he used his remaining balance for the wager.
Bookie Request: Nick jokingly asked Matt to place a $10K bet for him, to which Matt replied he'd do it if Nick Venmo'd him first, showcasing their banter around sports betting.

Hartford vs. Providence: Matt humorously compared Hartford to Providence, stating it's slightly better than Worcester, likening the comparison to "unkept buttholes," highlighting their inside jokes about cities.
Arc Raiders Excitement: Matt expressed a strong interest in playing Arc Raiders, mentioning he might buy it after his travels in January, and he encouraged others to join him in the game.

Protein Powder vs. Arc Raiders: Nic humorously compared the cost of Arc Raiders to his protein powder, noting he was on a strict gaming budget, which led to Matt suggesting he skip the protein to buy the game instead.
Nick's Enthusiasm: Nick downloaded Arc Raiders and was so impressed after the tutorial that he declared he wouldn't play anything else, prompting Matt to want to squad up with him.

BF6 Anticipation: Matt is extremely excited for the release of BF6, expressing that he might not play COD again once it comes out, and he believes it will be the greatest game ever made due to extensive gamer feedback.
Helldivers Pre-order: Matt has already pre-ordered Helldivers but jokes that he has a feeling no one else in the group will get it.
COD Critique: Matt criticizes COD for focusing on monetization through skins rather than improving gameplay, emphasizing his loyalty to the Battlefield franchise despite its past failures.
Nigel's Kojima Comment: In a humorous exchange, Matt jokes about not liking "putting things in my ass" in response to Nigel's suggestion that he might enjoy Hideo Kojima's realistic military games.
HLL Enthusiasm: Matt is very eager to play "Hell Let Loose" and encourages others to join him, often suggesting they ditch "COD" for it, emphasizing that it's free with Game Pass.
Group Gaming Dynamics: Nic admits they got beat up in a game but still had fun, highlighting the group's tendency to struggle in games together but enjoy the experience nonetheless.
Boys Trip Plans: Nick suggests a boys trip to NYC, and Matt is keen to coordinate a gaming session around it, specifically mentioning playing "Hell Let Loose" on Friday night.
Zombies Nostalgia: Matt expressed his longing for playing COD Zombies, indicating it's a favorite pastime, especially when Nick asked if he misses gaming.
Drunk Gaming: During a gaming session, Matt and Reed played Zombies, but they got "rekt," with Reed being notably drunk at the time.
Game Lobby Joke: Reed humorously noted that the game lobby had "too many cis white males in a lobby during pride month," prompting Matt to express his disdain for the game.
Gaming Plans: Matt frequently suggests playing Zombies with Reed, showing his enthusiasm for leveling up weapons like the Kar98k.
Black Ops 6 Anticipation: Matt is excited for Black Ops 6, joking about its massive file size and mentioning he plans to play it on both PC and Xbox when it releases.

RDR2 Online Plans: Matt expressed frustration that Reed hasn't reached out to play Red Dead Redemption 2 online, despite Reed averaging nearly 6 hours a day on the game without playing with Matt once.

Arthur vs. John Debate: A heated discussion arose between Matt and Reed about the importance of Arthur in John Marston's story, with Reed insisting that John would be dead without Arthur's influence, while Matt argued that RDR2 is primarily about setting up John's narrative.
Blind Drunk Horse Racing: Dave proposed a wild night of getting blind drunk and racing horses in the street, suggesting a chaotic gaming session with Reed.
Stacy's Scent: Reed joked that his girlfriend Stacy can "smell" when he's been flirting with other girls, referencing his escapades while discussing gaming plans.
Game Pass Enthusiasm: Matt frequently discusses his excitement about Game Pass, mentioning that he downloaded "BFV" and is looking forward to playing "Black Ops 6" on both Xbox and PC.
Roblox Confusion: Dave mistakenly invested hundreds of dollars in Robux thinking they were playing a Roblox zombie mod instead of the new game, leading to a humorous moment of realization.
Game Pass Premium Debate: Matt advises Dicky on whether to upgrade to Game Pass Premium or just buy a game outright, showcasing the group's ongoing discussions about gaming subscriptions and costs.
Gaming Plans: Matt expressed frustration about not being able to game with friends, especially during a tough week at work, and he offered to game with Reed if he showed up that night.

Warzone Love: Matt expressed his desire for more "Gweeods" in Warzone, emphasizing how much he misses playing with the group and even declared his love for Reed, prompting Reed to respond affirmatively.
Statue of Liberty: In a humorous exchange, Matt dismissed the Statue of Liberty while quoting a line from a Dropkick Murphys song, showcasing the group's playful banter about Boston and gaming plans.
"reed zacharias": This phrase is a frequent reference in the group, likely tied to an inside joke or story involving Reed Zacharias that has become a staple in their conversations.
"you guys": Matt often addresses the group with this phrase, indicating a casual and familiar tone in his interactions.
"nic dipierro": Nic is a recurring figure in Matt's messages, suggesting he plays a significant role in the group's dynamics or shared experiences.
"nick rondinone": Similar to Nic, Nick Rondinone is another key member of the group, frequently mentioned in Matt's conversations, hinting at their close-knit friendship.
"last night": This phrase indicates that Matt often recounts events or stories from the previous night, suggesting a lively social life and shared experiences with the group.
Political Identity: Matt describes himself as "socially liberal and fiscally conservative," indicating a blend of progressive social views with a more traditional approach to economic issues.

Social Media Critique: He believes social media is a significant threat to society, stating, "They are my public enemy #1 in terms of social media threats because they're our generation," reflecting a critical view of its impact on discourse and behavior.

TikTok's Economic Impact: Matt acknowledges TikTok's role in creating revenue streams for both corporations and independent entrepreneurs, stating, "TikTok has created an unreal amount of revenue streams from corporations all the way down to independent entrepreneurs."

Mental Health Awareness: He expresses concern about mental health issues, suggesting that certain behaviors in youth could lead to serious consequences, as he notes, "mental health is no joke and that kid is like the prime example of who we'd see on the news after a mass shooting."
Disdain for Warzone: Matt expresses frustration with the game Warzone, saying, "I hate playing it... it's absolutely beat to fucking shit," indicating a desire for new gaming experiences.

Taxation Sentiment: He expresses frustration with taxes, sharing that he had to pay a significant amount, saying, "I just cut the IRS a check for $3090," which reflects his discontent with the tax system.

Skepticism of National Media: Matt believes that national media is detrimental, stating, "They can be local or group social media entities but national scale just is bad news," indicating a preference for localized media over national narratives.

JFK Assassination: Matt finds the JFK assassination conspiracy theories interesting, stating, "JFK is cool until you put a bullet in the back of his head and try and put the blame on a single man who couldn't have fired the shots that hit the president."
9/11 Conspiracy Theories: He dismisses 9/11 conspiracy theories as "unfounded and not cool."
Gaming Attitude: Matt expresses a nonchalant attitude towards gaming losses, saying, "Definitely doesn't work me up like it used to, I could give two flying dick farts if I win or lose in that game."

Law and Order: Matt believes in strict enforcement of laws, expressing frustration that meth is illegal yet not being actively policed: "Dude why don't you just call the cops every time you see them smoke meth? Get those fuckers off the streets and let our hard earned tax paying dollars support them!"

Homelessness: He acknowledges the challenges of homelessness in Boston, noting that the city is brutal for homeless individuals and that shelters are limited: "In all reality, Boston is far too brutal to be homeless in."

Political Trials: Matt views the trial against Trump as a political maneuver that will ultimately backfire on the Democrats, stating, "this trial was a fucking sham, an absolutely brainless political ploy by the Democrats to 'prove nobody is above the law,' yet when all is said and done, there will be no jail time, no significant penalties, no justice for the 'crime.'"

Trump's Legal Troubles: He is confident that Trump will not face jail time, expressing frustration about the political climate: "I'm so fucking angry about all this I really don't want to even give it any more thought or airtime. NOTHING IS CHANGING IN AMERICA AFTER THIS."

Hunter Biden's Charges: Matt expresses satisfaction about Hunter Biden's felony gun charges, suggesting it will create a difficult narrative for the Right if Biden does not pardon him: "I really am thrilled about this because it's going to be a pretty strong statement to see him get a prison sentence and then Biden doesn't pardon his own son."

Economics: Matt feels that his financial situation has deteriorated, stating, "Katie and I used to feel like we were rapidly approaching that upper middle class lifestyle... and we don't have as much financial freedom as we thought we would have at this point in our lives. All because of Biden."
Cost of Living: He expresses frustration about the rising cost of living, noting, "Our income has decreased 30% in the last year while cost of living has increased by like 11%."
Political Candidates: Matt believes that "Biden needs to step aside and they need a new candidate in the next 30 days," indicating dissatisfaction with the current leadership.

Social Issues: He was disturbed by a bigoted chant he witnessed at a baseball game, saying, "I was so disappointed and I couldn't believe what I was hearing and seeing," and he criticized the crowd for supporting the bigot.

Cultural Commentary: Matt sarcastically remarks on the irony of needing to donate to wish Trump a happy birthday, stating, "It's so funny you can't wish Trump a Happy Birthday without donating to his and Marsha Blackburn's campaign."

Radical Political Views: He jokingly claims to be creating a "radical liberal faction called the 'Cuck Boys'" and suggests a violent response to political issues, saying, "if Trump wins we're staging a January 6th 2.0 where we actually use guns and explosives to wipe all the scum out."

Violence Against Trump: Matt expresses a strong desire for violence against Trump, stating, "I want that man dead. I would celebrate if his brains got blown all over his supporters in the background."

Assassination Rhetoric: He believes that the situation surrounding the assassination attempt on Trump could be spun to boost his support, saying, "This whole situation sucks. It went from possibly being the best thing that's ever happened to America to being another boost for Trump's support."
Disgust with Rhetoric: Matt criticizes the rhetoric blaming the left for the assassination attempt, calling it "pretty disgusting."
Support for Kamala Harris: He expresses hope that Kamala Harris will effectively challenge Trump in debates, saying, "I really hope Kamala shreds Trump to pieces tonight."

Cynicism Towards Media: Matt shows skepticism towards the media's portrayal of events, suggesting that he wouldn't put it past Trump to stage incidents for political gain, stating, "Honestly I wouldn't put it past that shitbag."
Concerns About Privacy: He expresses concern about data privacy, stating, "Meta sells our data to China," indicating distrust in how tech companies handle personal information.
China and Russia's Influence: Matt believes that China is trying to influence U.S. elections to favor Kamala Harris, while Russia is doing the same to support Trump.

Voting Record: Matt voted early and supported a range of progressive measures, including the legalization of psychedelics and the removal of the MCAS as a high school graduation requirement, but opposed unionization for rideshare drivers and raising the minimum wage for tipped workers, citing polling data that suggests these changes are not desired by the affected workers.
Societal Decline: He expresses concern about societal decline, particularly criticizing the attention given to certain social media figures, which he finds mind-blowing.

Inflation and Leadership: Matt questions whether Kamala Harris is genuinely looking out for the best interests of Americans, pointing to inflation and border issues as concerns, suggesting a comparison to Trump's leadership.

Healthcare System: "Fuck the Healthcare system" and "I hope that whole corporation crumbles," expressing strong disdain for the current state of healthcare in America, particularly criticizing United Healthcare.

New York City: "NYC is such a fucking shithole" and "I'm genuinely unbothered by that news and really couldn't care less," indicating a critical view of the city's safety and governance, while also expressing indifference towards violent incidents.

Sicily vs. Mainland Italy: "I saw more poverty in Sicily than I did on the main land without question," suggesting a nuanced perspective on regional disparities in Italy, with a specific observation about poverty levels.
Warzone Gaming: "The new Warzone is absolute ass garbage," showing a strong negative opinion about the latest iteration of the game, while also expressing a desire to play multiplayer instead.

Humor about Hunter Biden: "That's going to be at its namesake's bar in the future!" and "Of course we sell Slopshits! That'll be a registered trademark," indicating a humorous take on the controversy surrounding Hunter Biden, while also engaging in a light-hearted banter about a fictional bar.

Presidential Rankings: Matt believes that Jimmy Carter, Donald Trump, and Joe Biden are among the worst presidents in American history, stating, "Carter, Trump, and Biden are all towards the bottom of the barrel in terms of Presidential Greatness."
Obama's Legacy: He thinks Obama's greatness will become more apparent over time, saying, "I think Obama's greatness is going to become more apparent as we move on in history."
George Washington: Matt has a strong negative opinion about George Washington, expressing disdain with, "FUCK GEORGE WASHINGTON... HE DIDN'T DO SHIT."

Critique of the Left: He criticizes the left for their "constant knee-jerk reactions" and "bitching and whining" about Trump, suggesting that the majority of America wanted Trump and that complaining won't change anything.

Military Comparison: Matt states that "France's military is 21% larger than Italy's," indicating a belief in the importance of military size, though he acknowledges that size alone doesn't equate to quality.
TikTok Ban: "I 1000% believe America is better off without TikTok but I also hate the Costco Guys so what do I know?"

Social Media's Impact: "I don't want them making political decisions about quality of life, social policies and economic policies that matter, social media doesn't matter in the grand scheme of things."
Data Privacy: "I'm fine with my data being exposed, it's already happened and nothing is going to stop it."
Government Efficiency: "Maybe the government inefficiencies getting cleaned up will fix that?" (in response to a discussion about job vacancies)
China's Threat: "My brother in law who works for the Office of Naval Intelligence... swears by Signal and has been telling me to use that instead of Messaging Apps and WhatsApp for years."
Military-Industrial Complex: Matt seems to support the ongoing operations of the military-industrial complex, expressing enthusiasm for American business savvy in military parts production.

Federal Government and DEI Initiatives: He acknowledges the backlash against DEI (Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion) initiatives in the federal government, noting that his sister faced repercussions related to it.

Trump's Impact on Jobs: Matt expresses a desire for people who voted for Trump to face job losses as a consequence of his policies, indicating a belief that Trump's actions will negatively affect many Americans.

Critique of Federal Grants: He sarcastically refers to federal grants as "liberal handouts" and expresses frustration with government waste, suggesting a critical view of how federal funding is utilized.

America's Foreign Policy: Matt critiques Trump's approach to foreign policy, referring to it as "America the bully," and expresses concern about the implications of Trump's actions on international relations.
Skepticism Towards Texas: He dismisses Texas as a potential travel destination, indicating a negative view of the state without elaborating on specific reasons.

Democracy: Matt believes that democracy shouldn't count when a significant portion of the electorate is uninformed, stating, "Democracy shouldn't count when 50% of the people voting are fucking idiots."

Trump Voters: He expresses a strong disdain for Trump voters, saying, "Hard to say I'm getting along with anyone who's a Trump voter right now," but clarifies that he doesn't hate individuals who voted for Trump, just the current state of America and the beliefs of those who think things are going well under Trump.

DOGE and Data Privacy: Matt criticizes the fear-mongering around financial data privacy, arguing that concerns about who has access to tax information are overblown, as he believes this data is already accessible to the government. He states, "there are MUCH larger issues with DOGE that Americans need to be aware of."

Gender Commentary: He made a controversial remark about women being "wildly emotional and erratic when on their periods," which he defended in a discussion about a phrase he used, indicating a complicated and potentially problematic view on gender and emotional responses.

DNC Nomination: Matt wishes the DNC had nominated Bernie Sanders instead of Hillary Clinton, expressing frustration with the party's decision-making that he feels disregarded the American people's interests.
Vermont Politics: He expresses disdain for Vermont politics, calling it "horrible," indicating a critical view of local governance there.
Trump's Intelligence: Matt believes Trump is "not very bright," suggesting that he lacks the capability to make wise decisions, particularly in the context of sports team selections.

Medicaid and Trump Supporters: He finds irony in Trump supporters relying on Medicaid, suggesting that many of them may face dire consequences if Medicaid is cut, and expresses a harsh sentiment about their potential suffering.

Economic Outlook: Matt is convinced that the global economy is going to crash and is critical of the current administration's handling of economic issues, specifically blaming Trump for worsening the economy.

World War III Concerns: He does not believe that World War III is imminent, arguing that the necessity of the U.S. in global affairs prevents such a conflict, despite acknowledging the embarrassment of current U.S. behavior.

Critique of Tax Protest Movement: Matt finds humor in a movement where people are considering not paying taxes to demonstrate the impact on Trump's administration, questioning the feasibility of such a protest.

Criticism of Trump's Trade Policies: He acknowledges that while Trump is bullying the EU into favorable trade deals, this behavior is making the U.S. more disliked globally, reflecting a nuanced view of international relations.
Stock Market Manipulation: Matt believes that Biden's actions have artificially propped up the stock market, leading to a "normal correction" that he views as inevitable.

Military-Industrial Complex (MIC): He thinks that JFK was assassinated because he wanted to withdraw from Vietnam and reduce US-Soviet tensions, which would have been detrimental to the profits of the MIC.

Tariffs and Trade Deficit: Matt argues that the trade deficit resulting from tariffs is misunderstood, explaining that the US predominantly exports services that do not incur the same logistical costs as manufactured goods, making tariffs less applicable.

Protests and Public Sentiment: He expresses skepticism about the motivations of protesters, suggesting that many are more focused on issues like "Free Palestine" than on pressing domestic concerns, and criticizes their lack of awareness regarding the real problems facing the country.
Critique of Elon Musk: Matt labels Elon Musk a "NAZI" and expresses disdain for the relevance of protests against him, arguing that Musk's situation is already dire and the protests are misguided.

Economic Outlook: He describes his financial situation as dire, noting that his portfolio and 401k are suffering while the cost of living continues to rise, indicating a pessimistic view of the current economic climate.

Economics: Matt believes that Trump's policies are negatively impacting both the stock and crypto markets, stating, "Trump isn't just tanking the stock market, he's taltanking the crypto market too!!!!"

Economics: He expresses a strong opinion on Tesla's future, declaring, "Tesla is done, their stock is in shambles, their supply chain is getting priced through the roof, and people aren't buying Teslas anymore."

Foreign Policy: Matt warns about the potential consequences of Trump's tariffs, suggesting that "the whole world has the ability to force the United States to default on their debts and destroy their credit rating" if Trump continues his current path.

Media: He uses X (formerly Twitter) strategically to expose himself to right-wing content for the sake of understanding their viewpoints, saying, "I get plenty of rightwing/conservative bullshit popping up on my 'For You' feed that I use for my 'getting triggered/view into their asshole base.'"
Social Issues: Matt expresses a strong aversion to tofu, calling it "disgusting" and stating, "it's un-American to eat disgusting food so I'm gonna stick with chicken, pork, and beef!"

Political Leadership: Matt believes that if Trump runs for president again, Obama will also run, and he expresses confidence that Obama would answer the call to lead again, stating, "I believe in him."

Patriots and Politics: While Matt expresses a strong love for the New England Patriots, he acknowledges that he would criticize Robert Kraft if he were using the team's plane for political purposes, specifically mentioning Trump's "dirty work."

Foreign Policy: Matt shows a dismissive attitude towards the conflict between India and Pakistan, commenting, "Welp India and Pakistan seem to be at war now...that's cool," indicating a lack of concern or urgency about the situation.

Social Issues: He expresses a negative view towards a political figure by stating, "I know he's anti-gay and anti-abortion and that's enough for me to know about him," suggesting that these positions are disqualifying for him.
Critique of Government: Matt critiques the IRS, stating, "I can tell you confidently the IRS won't do anything to help you," reflecting skepticism about government efficiency and support systems.

Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) Programs: Matt is frustrated with SAP's decision to eliminate their DEI program, viewing it as a capitulation to legal challenges that ultimately changes nothing and only serves as an optics move. He expresses a loss of respect for his employer over this decision.

Protests and Political Accountability: Matt believes that as long as Trump is in power, he can ignore the consequences of his actions, including protests. He feels that protests won't make a difference until there's a significant shift in power, which he doesn't foresee happening soon.

Societal Issues: Matt asserts that America is a racist, sexist, xenophobic, and homo/transphobic society that has allowed Trump to validate hate speech. He believes that the country is revealing its true colors and that this is not surprising.

Foreign Policy and Israel: Matt expresses a strong negative opinion about Israel, suggesting that they are receiving "a taste of their own medicine" for their actions in Gaza. He criticizes Israel for killing innocent civilians while portraying themselves as victims.

Military-Industrial Complex (MIC): Matt argues that there is no need to support the war effort because the military-industrial complex has been prepared for war for decades, indicating skepticism about the motivations behind military actions and spending.

Western Intervention in Islamic Nations: Matt believes that Western civilizations have historically set up leadership factions in Islamic nations to serve their interests, only to abandon them, which leads to civil unrest and radicalization. He finds this dynamic important to consider when discussing the state of these nations.
Tucker Carlson: While Matt expresses a strong dislike for Tucker Carlson, he acknowledges that Carlson's recent actions in making Ted Cruz look foolish were indeed hilarious.

Cuomo and Elections: Matt is critical of the political landscape in NYC, stating that elections can be bought and expressing skepticism about Andrew Cuomo's potential to win, while also advocating for a candidate like Mamdani who represents real change against the establishment.

Healthcare and Food Assistance: Matt is frustrated that the right will likely blame immigrants and Joe Biden for the suffering of people in red states once they lose access to healthcare and food assistance programs, indicating a belief that the political narrative will shift to avoid accountability.
TikTok: Matt strongly believes that TikTok is bad and dangerous, expressing a clear opposition to the platform.

Anger Over Recent Legislation: Matt expresses intense anger over a recent bill that passed, feeling it was a missed opportunity for Trump to falter and indicating that he believes the Senate's actions will worsen the situation in the House.

9/11 and Political Commentary: Matt makes a provocative statement linking the events of 9/11 to current political dynamics, suggesting that the memory of the attacks is being forgotten in the face of new political leadership, which he views as a dangerous oversight.
Lisa Murkowski: Matt describes Lisa Murkowski as "spineless," indicating a strong disapproval of her political stance or actions.

Red States and Political Strategy: He anticipates that red states will suffer due to recent legislation while blaming immigrants and the Biden administration, suggesting a belief that political strategies will shift blame rather than address the root causes of issues.

Fear-Based Politics: Matt criticizes the left for using fear-based politics instead of presenting actionable solutions, stating, "this is 1000% bullshit and a really good example of the left's fear-based politics they've been hiding behind rather than taking action on anything."

Corporate Political Influence: He argues that just because corporations do business with political entities doesn't mean their business dealings are inherently political, saying, "If you looked up every major corporation that's done business with the RNC... that doesn't mean their business dealings have become political."

Reputation of Corporations: Matt believes that companies like Oracle would not compromise their reputation for political appeasement, stating, "BUT companies like Oracle would never sacrifice their reputation just to appease one base."

Distrust in Political Institutions: He expresses skepticism about the motivations of both major political parties, suggesting that they protect their own interests over addressing serious issues, saying, "At the end of the day it's just millionaires and billionaires helping each other out and it has nothing to do with politics or sex trafficking crimes."
Support for Newsom: Matt expresses support for Gavin Newsom as a presidential candidate, stating, "Newsom should run for President."

Respect for Open Dialogue: He respects individuals who entertain views from both sides rather than immediately dismissing them, saying, "I respect anyone who will entertain both sides' views rather than default to assaulting or denying them immediately."
Critique of Trump's America: Matt describes the current political climate under Trump as one where "Fascist, Racist, homophobic, xenophobic, Islamophobic, its all acceptable in Trump's America!"
Indifference to Epstein List: He expresses a lack of concern about the Epstein List, stating, "In all honesty guys, I really don't care about the Epstein List."
Critique of Marriage: Matt views marriage as primarily a financial arrangement, saying, "Marriage is an institution, only benefit is the tax break, that's it."

Government Regulation of Substances: He advocates for minimal government intervention regarding personal choices like smoking or vaping, stating, "Honestly I dont think that should have to do with politics, if you wanna smoke or vape keep the government's hands off!"

Political Identity: Matt does not consider himself strictly aligned with either major political party, stating, "I don't consider myself an either or guy, you need both and if you don't have both I'm not interested."

Economic Perspective: He expressed frustration about someone thinking $60 was a lot of money given their occupation, jokingly stating, "so I'm now a Republican..." indicating a nuanced view on economic discussions and personal finance.
Foreign Policy: After visiting Burlington, Matt suggested, "Trump should send the troops there tbh..." which reflects a critical and somewhat sarcastic view on military intervention.

Cultural Commentary: He remarked on the absurdity of a rumor linking political preferences to physical preferences, saying, "That makes zero sense," showcasing his skepticism towards oversimplified political stereotypes.

Boston's Gentrification: Matt believes Boston has become a "disgusting display of wealth and privilege" due to gentrification and rent gouging, leading to a loss of the city's identity and making it "sterile and lifeless."
Political Corruption: He criticizes politicians in Boston as "phony ideological high grounders" who pretend to care for the people while being examples of political corruption.
Democratic Hypocrisy: Matt expresses frustration with the "phony" Democrats and liberals in Massachusetts, suggesting they contribute to the rampant issues of late-stage capitalism.
Charlie Kirk's Influence: He sees Charlie Kirk as a significant figure in swinging the 2024 election and believes he has radicalized hate among younger generations.

Gun Violence Stance: Matt states he does not believe people should be allowed to "gun people down in cold blood and not face consequences," indicating a nuanced view on gun rights that includes criticism of violence.
Distrust in Media Figures: He suggests that Charlie Kirk's identity might not be real and views him as a "made character to spread fake news," reflecting skepticism about media narratives.

Critique of Corporate Influence: Matt is critical of how the Trump family has amassed wealth, questioning why MAGA supporters do not scrutinize the financial benefits that US policy and government dealings have provided to them.

Social Media's Impact: He believes TikTok is detrimental to American society, stating it is "garbage rotting America's brains," while also expressing concern about the overwhelming amount of content on Instagram.
Healthcare Funding: Matt believes the narrative that Democrats are fighting for healthcare for illegal immigrants is "absolutely absurd."
Government Shutdown: He thinks the Democrats are "holding the country hostage" to fund government initiatives, specifically criticizing their focus on funding for illegal immigrants' trans surgeries.
Tourism and Rent Control: Matt understands the perspective on rent control in tourist-heavy cities like Barcelona but did not observe much animosity towards tourists during his visit.
Cultural Identity: He expresses a desire to identify more with his Portuguese and Irish heritage rather than his French ancestry, despite acknowledging his French roots.
European vs. American Work Benefits: He admires the decent pay, paid healthcare, and family benefits that sanitation workers in Barcelona receive, contrasting it with American work culture.
Blending Cultures: Matt wishes for a blend of the best parts of Europe with the best of America to create an ideal country.

Media: Matt criticizes the New York Times as a "fucking liberal ass" outlet, suggesting he finds their science-based articles obvious and not a concern unless one spends excessive time at indoor shooting ranges.

Economics: Matt expresses skepticism about companies' slower migration strategies in technology, arguing that they ultimately lead to higher costs and more complexity, emphasizing the importance of Enterprise Architecture.

Economics: He believes the global economy is likely to crash in the Spring, citing Michael Burry's strategy of shorting major tech and AI stocks, which he thinks are overvalued and not accounting for rapid depreciation.

Social Issues: Matt supports the idea that "Katie's body, her choice," indicating his belief in bodily autonomy and the right to make personal decisions regarding family planning without external pressure.
Clinton and Trump: Matt expresses a desire to see both Bill Clinton and Donald Trump face consequences for their actions, indicating a lack of trust in both figures.
Obama's Presidency: He believes Obama was "probably the greatest President of our lifetime" and sees a stark contrast between Obama and the Clintons.
Bill Clinton's Character: While he appreciated Bill Clinton as a politician, he calls him a "POS scumbag," showing a complex view that separates political effectiveness from personal morality.
Taxation and Government Spending: Matt is willing to pay taxes but insists they should be used for "good use," implying dissatisfaction with current government spending.
Crypto Transactions: He believes that crypto transactions don't need to be reported unless converted back to USD, indicating a pragmatic approach to tax obligations in the crypto space.

Amy Schumer's Weight Loss: Matt criticizes Amy Schumer for hypocrisy regarding body image, noting her previous stance against body shaming while now celebrating her weight loss transformation, but acknowledges her right to make choices about her body.

Immigration: Matt believes that true patriotism involves embracing the country's immigrant roots, stating, "You don't love a country founded and built by immigrants by turning your back on them and scapegoating them for the wrongs corporate greed has created in a broken system."

Trump's Ideals: He asserts that anyone who aligns with Trump's ideals does not truly love America, saying, "I refuse to believe that anyone that stands with Trump's ideals loves America, they don't know what America is if that's the case."
Israel's Right to Defend Itself: Matt expresses support for Israel's right to defend itself, indicating a nuanced stance on foreign policy.
Critique of ICE: He criticizes the hiring practices of ICE, agreeing with a friend that the agency's training is a "fucking joke" and commenting on the incompetence of its personnel.

Political Violence: Matt reacts to police violence, suggesting that it has become the "new norm," and expresses skepticism about accountability for those involved, saying, "He won't face a lick of punishment... Probably get the Presidential Medal of Freedom."
Media and Racism: He reflects on the racism present in legacy media and artists, stating, "Turns out a lot of legacy media and artists are racists!"
Foreign Policy: Matt believes that "Israel has a right to defend itself," expressing strong support for Israel's actions in conflict situations.

Media and Politics: He acknowledges that philanthropic donations by billionaires, like Robert Kraft's contributions, are often just tax offsets, showing a critical understanding of the motivations behind such actions.

Work Culture: Matt critiques the inefficiency in companies like SAP, suggesting that many employees do not contribute meaningfully to their work, indicating a belief in a more meritocratic work environment.

Sports and National Identity: He emphasizes that his interest in men's hockey during the Olympics is purely about athletic achievement and national representation, distancing it from political affiliations or implications.
Social Issues: He expresses concern about the training and effectiveness of border patrol agents, implying a belief that they should be better equipped to handle their responsibilities.

Hockey Rivalry: Matt believes the USA-Canada hockey rivalry is at its peak, comparable to the USA-USSR Cold War era, and he loves the intensity of it. He acknowledges that the rivalry was fueled by both talent and global political fear, but he does not view Soviet players as "bad guys," instead recognizing them as representatives of their country.
Women in Sports: Matt expresses skepticism about the opportunities for women in sports, stating, "There is no fighting chance for women unless you're wearing USA or Canada on your sweater."

Corporate Critique: While he criticizes corporate overlords, Matt paradoxically appreciates the benefits they provide, saying, "Serving up corpo slop so you can consume capitalism slop... Slop is good." He also acknowledges that the economy is heavily reliant on AI, weight loss drugs, and sports betting.
Costco Support: Matt expresses strong support for Costco, stating, "Costco is what big retail should be IMO," and humorously claims, "I'll go to war for Costco. I'll die for Costco."

Political Disillusionment: Matt is cynical about the legal system's ability to hold Trump accountable, saying, "Trump is above the law and that's just a fact." He also expresses disappointment in the presence of right-wing figures in hockey, particularly criticizing the Tkachuk brothers for their association with Trump.

Military-Industrial Complex: Matt warns about the dangers of the military-industrial complex, stating that it has been called out for nearly 100 years without any meaningful change, and he believes that profiteering is driving nations towards war.

Foreign Policy: Matt believes "Israel has a right to defend itself," but also expresses a surprising sympathy for Islamic states, stating, "Never in a million years did I think I would sympathize with Islamic states but Jesus christ are they looking like the protagonists every day."

Economics: He critiques the current economic situation, sarcastically stating, "THANKS BIDEN," and highlights the recent downturn in the stock market, indicating dissatisfaction with the economic performance under the current administration.

Social Issues: Matt makes a humorous yet pointed comment about Kristi Noem, saying, "I say this with 100% consideration of intolerance which I'm intolerant of, but she belongs in the kitchen," reflecting a complex view on gender roles and political figures.

Media Critique: He expresses disdain for corporate culture and media, stating, "executives who have absolutely zero fucking clue what our software does because they can only consume corpo slop," indicating a critical view of corporate America and its disconnect from reality.

War and Conflict: He argues that the ongoing war is "far more damaging to the American public and the world than the protection of a billionaire pedophile ring," suggesting a prioritization of war impacts over other scandals like the Epstein case.
